title: Proclamation No. 40, s. 1936
text:
MALACAÑAN PALACE MANILA

PROCLAMATION NO. 40

REVOKING PROCLAMATION NO. 127, SERIES OF 1927, AND RESERVING AND DESIGNATING AS PAMABARAN BURI FOREST RESERVE FOR FOREST PROTECTION AND CONSERVATION OF BURI PALMS THE PARCEL OF PUBLIC DOMAIN SITUATED IN THE MUNICIPALITIES OF SAN ANTONIO AND CABIAO, PROVINCE OF NUEVA ECIJA, AND MUNICIPALITY OF CONCEPCION, PROVINCE OF TARLAC, ISLAND OF LUZON.

Upon the recommendation of the Director of Forestry, approved by the Secretary of Agriculture and Commerce, I, Manuel L. Quezon, President of the Philippines, hereby revoke Proclamation Numbered One hundred twenty-seven, series of nineteen hundred and twenty-seven, classifying as timber land a parcel of the public domain situated in the Municipalities of San Antonio and Cabiao, Province of Nueva Ecija, Municipality of Concepcion, Province of Tarlac, and in the Municipality of Magalang, Province of Pampanga, Island of Luzon; and pursuant to the provisions of section eighteen hundred and twenty-six of Act Numbered Twenty-seven hundred and eleven, known as the Revised Administrative Code, I hereby establish the Pamabaran Buri Forest Reserve and place it under the administration and control of the Bureau of Forestry, which shall have the authority to regulate the cutting, collection, and removal of buri palms and other forest products therein in accordance with the Forest Law and regulations for forest protection and conservation of buri palms, subject to private rights, if any there be, and to any and all rights and privileges of exploration, occupation, purchase, or otherwise granted or reserved to citizens by provisions of the Act of Congress of July first’, nineteen hundred and two, known as the Philippine Bill.

I, therefore, withdraw from sale or settlement, except as permitted or provided for in the aforesaid Act of Congress of July first, nineteen hundred and two, and hereby declare and proclaim to be the Pamabaran Buri Forest Reserve that portion of the public domain situated in the Municipalities of San Antonio and Cabiao, Province of Nueva Ecija, and Municipality of Concepcion, Province of Tarlac, Island of Luzon, and described in the Bureau of Forestry map No. P. R.-59, to wit:
